metrics.narrative: Bosch's technical metrics are explicit and quantified in Salesforce MEDDPICC (SLA performance, scalability to 10,000 concurrent users and 11M invoices/year, GDPR/four-eyes/SOX/audit compliance, AI accuracy), and these were substantively engaged on the 11 Mar 2026 Technical Deep Dive, where Zycus committed to 99.5% uptime (99.98% average), a 12-hour RTO / 2-hour RPO for the S2P suite, and a ≤10-second API response target. No quantified financial ROI or cost-savings case has been voiced by a Bosch stakeholder in any read call, so the business-value side of the metric remains unconfirmed.

champion.narrative: Thomas Kirmse is named champion in Salesforce MEDDPICC ('too early to say but... open for calls and additional communication') and has been Zycus's central RFP contact since 20 Jan 2026, including a direct mobile call with the deal owner on 12 May 2026. No evidence shows him actively brokering Economic Buyer access or advocating internally beyond his own category-manager role; the plan to use him to reach the economic buyer post-POC has not been confirmed as executed.

competition.narrative: Bosch confirmed running a formal multi-vendor process (a stated long-list of 4-5 suppliers as of 2 Feb 2026), but no specific rival has been named in any Salesforce field or any of the 21 deep-read calls; the Competitors__c field itself is unpopulated. Zycus's relative standing in the field cannot be confirmed beyond continuing to be invited into RFP Round 2.

identify_pain.narrative: Bosch's pain is well documented both in the CRM (fragmented procurement and systems, limited automation, poor UX, non-standardized processes, need for AI and scalability) and echoed repeatedly across the POC calls, including a description of an 'analytics nightmare from fragmented best-of-breed tools' and manual, email/Excel-driven supplier and bid processes at the 11 May 2026 kick-off. This pain was the explicit basis for the 33-use-case POC scope.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifacts (MSA, Order Form, SOW) have surfaced in any source; the deal is still in the RFP/evaluation stage, which is appropriate for its Shortlisted position but means paper process has not meaningfully started. The 31 Jul 2026 close date leaves no visible runway for a paper process once RFP Round 2 closes on 17 Jul 2026.

economic_buyer.narrative: An Economic Buyer is named in Salesforce MEDDPICC 2.0, so Access to Power is on record as identified. No call or note in the read evidence shows this person directly engaging with Zycus, however: the 12 May 2026 Next Step floated a plan to 'engage with c-level once we are through the current stage of testing,' and as of the 8 Jun 2026 update (Bosch stakeholders on PTO, following up on pricing/contracts), that step still had not happened.

decision_process.narrative: Bosch is running a structured, multi-round RFP: RFI submitted 3 Dec 2025, RFP invitation 12 Jan 2026, a stated long-list of 4-5 suppliers as of 2 Feb 2026, a two-week on-site POC completed 26 May 2026, and RFP Round 2 documents received 29 Jun 2026 with a response due 17 Jul 2026. The 22 May 2026 closing meeting indicated commercial/contract discussions would follow 'in June,' but the 8 Jun 2026 Next Step shows that follow-up stalling (Bosch team on PTO), so the process is live but has lost pace since the POC ended.

decision_criteria.narrative: Salesforce MEDDPICC names four explicit criteria: functional depth and AI innovation, user-friendliness, integration capability, and innovation potential. These were validated point-by-point in the Bosch delivery team's own closing assessment (via Rebecca) on 22 May 2026, which walked through strengths and gaps module by module and tied specific findings (e.g. Merlin intake strength, integration capability gap) directly back to these criteria.

day_summary.overall: The PoC phase officially closed out today via email — Bosch's Sabina Nordmann confirmed internal alignment is complete and no further PoC-scope meetings are needed, while Zycus's Asmita Mukherjee committed to sending Zycus's own response to Bosch's POC evaluation summary by end of week. The ball is now in Zycus's court to deliver that written response before next steps (likely commercial/contract discussions) can begin.

critical_signals.text: The recorded 31 July close date has essentially no runway behind it: the RFP response isn't even due until 17 July, and this account's own process typically needs another month or more to move from a submitted proposal to a signature.

The most recent deep technical session ended with the buyer's integration lead saying flatly that the deal 'wasn't moving forward' on outstanding documentation, and the Bosch side warned they would reassess their evaluation based on that outcome.
No rival has ever been named to Zycus by name across the entire evaluation, even though Bosch has confirmed running a formal multi-vendor process since February; the field remains a black box.
A written commitment on two items Bosch itself called mandatory for security sign-off has been outstanding for roughly six weeks with no confirmed delivery.
No one at the economic-buyer or executive level on the customer side has ever spoken directly with Zycus, despite a plan floated back in mid-May to make that introduction once testing wrapped up.

customer_preference.evidence: The 22 May 2026 closing review was detailed, constructive and generally warm ('a very solid foundation'), and Bosch continued the process into RFP Round 2 (documents received 29 Jun 2026), but no Bosch stakeholder has explicitly stated a preference for Zycus over any rival.

competitive_position.summary: Bosch has run a formal, blind multi-vendor RFP since late 2025: an initial long-list of 4-5 suppliers (Feb 2026) narrowed Zycus to Shortlisted, and RFP Round 2 materials landed 29 Jun 2026 with a 17 Jul 2026 response deadline. No rival vendor has ever been named in any call, field, or Next Step entry, so the current threat is unnamed and unranked; the strongest available signal is that Zycus survived to Round 2 after a strongly-reviewed POC (22 May 2026).

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Bosch S2P is in the RFP evaluation phase (Shortlisted), coming off an unusually deep, well-documented two-week on-site POC (11-26 May 2026) that validated broad platform fit but also surfaced unresolved compliance and integration gaps. Engagement has cooled since the POC closed: the last confirmed two-way buyer touch was an email received 2 Jun 2026, and the only recent forward movement is RFP Round 2 documents logged 29 Jun 2026, due 17 Jul 2026, eight days from today.

deal_risk.text: The 31 Jul 2026 close date is not credible: RFP Round 2 is not due until 17 Jul 2026, leaving essentially no runway for decision, negotiation and contracting before the recorded close date.

BYOK and SCIM identity architecture, called a Bosch 'mandate' and 'critical deviation' on 22 May 2026, with a written commitment promised for around 29 May 2026, remains undelivered roughly six weeks later.
API documentation (Swagger, Postman collection, sample JSON payloads) requested by Lisa-Anne Walterspacher on 26 May 2026 to validate Integration Capability has not been confirmed as delivered.
Bosch's preferred implementation partner, EY, cannot support the engagement, and a replacement partner list flagged as needed since 8 May 2026 has no confirmed delivery in later evidence.

win_position.text: Bosch ran Zycus through an unusually deep, structured POC (11-26 May 2026) spanning S2C, P2O, integration and AI use cases, and the Bosch delivery team's own closing assessment (22 May 2026, via Rebecca) called the platform 'a very solid foundation' with 'a very functional end to end sourcing process.'

Decision criteria are explicit and were validated point-by-point in that same closing review (functional depth/AI, UX, integration, innovation potential), with the platform earning specific praise for Merlin intake guidance, contract-negotiation AI, and demand-management groundwork.

No economic buyer or C-level Bosch contact has directly engaged with Zycus; the plan floated in the 12 May 2026 Next Step ('engage with c-level once we are through testing') was never executed even after the POC closed on 22 May 2026 -- Access to Power remains unconfirmed beyond the named EB on file.

The 22 May 2026 closing review flagged two 'critical deviations' -- missing BYOK/SCIM identity architecture (called a Bosch mandate) and no API documentation -- both of which remain undelivered as of this sweep, directly threatening the Integration Capability decision criterion.

Competition is unnamed -- a 4-5 supplier long-list was cited back in February 2026 but no rival has been identified by name in any call or CRM field since, so relative standing versus the field cannot be confirmed.

deal_momentum.text: The deal has cooled materially since the POC closed 22 May 2026: the only confirmed two-way buyer contact since is an inbound email on 2 Jun 2026, and the next dated step (RFP Round 2 documents, logged 29 Jun 2026) is a rep-logged update, not a fresh buyer conversation.
RFP Round 2 carries a hard, dated deadline (17 Jul 2026, ~8 days from today), keeping the deal on a live, structured evaluation clock rather than open-ended silence.

A planned call between the deal owner and Thomas Kirmse for 25 Jun 2026 (logged in Next Step History on 22 Jun 2026) has no confirmed outcome in any later record heading into the RFP Round 2 deadline.

Commercial discussions Bosch said would resume 'in June' (22 May 2026 closing meeting) appear stalled -- the 8 Jun 2026 next-step note records Bosch stakeholders on PTO with pricing/contract follow-up still pending.

customer_commitment.text: Bosch invested unusually heavily in the evaluation: a two-day on-site kickoff (11-12 May 2026), daily check-ins through 22 May 2026, and a dedicated AI deep-dive and closing review, with multiple stakeholders (Sabina Nordmann, Lisa-Anne Walterspacher, Bruno Streitenberger, Andre Hoepfinger) actively testing and giving detailed, constructive feedback.
Zycus's regional VP joined the 26 May 2026 technical session in person, matching Bosch's senior stakeholder presence and signalling executive-level investment from the Zycus side.

Buyer tone cooled on unresolved technical asks: Lisa-Anne Walterspacher pressed repeatedly for API documentation on the 26 May 2026 call and closed with 'we are not moving forward somehow,' while Bruno Streitenberger stated Bosch 'will reevaluate our evaluation' based on that meeting's outcome.

Current indirect procurement lacks efficiency, automation, data consistency, supplier risk transparency, and user experience. Bosch wants one unified, modern, scalable S2P platform.' Comments_on_Qualification: 'Entire S2P project global'. Shortlisted stage with LastActivityDate 11 May 2026 indicating active late-stage engagement.

Business_Objectives is logged only as 'Done' without AI specifics, and Description is blank. Three AI modules in scope show product-level AI footprint but boundary defaulted DOWN at 6/7 without explicit prospect-side AI champion or selection criterion in allowed evidence. Score 6 reflects rich AI scope + platform-consolidation narrative without AI-as-selection-criterion.

deltas.to: Close BYOK/SCIM InfoSec gate immediately: Bosch flagged BYOK and SCIM as 'critical deviations' and 'mandate' 22 May; Varun committed written confirmation by 29
deltas.subject: Close BYOK/SCIM InfoSec gate immediately: Bosch flagged BYOK and SCIM as 'critical deviations' and 'mandate' 22 May; Varun committed written confirmation by 29 May, now overdue 5+ weeks with no delivery logged. InfoSec cannot award without closure. Competitor offering native BYOK/SCIM wins on decision criterion #3 (Integration Capability). Rank-1 move: deliver written BYOK/SCIM commitment + implementation roadmap to Thomas Kirmse by 10 Jul (3 days before RFP Round 2 due date) to neutralize risk.

deltas.subject: Secure C-level (CPO/CFO) intro before competitor: No C-level engagement recorded; Next_Step 12 May stated 'engage with c level once through testing' but POC closed 22 May with no exec intro actioned. Thomas Kirmse gates access but has not made intro. Competitor securing C-level sponsorship first wins executive alignment. Rank-2 move: ask Kirmse (via John Woodcock exec connect) to facilitate CPO/CFO intro in July to validate Merlin AI business case and align on Aug 2026 project start timeline.

deltas.to: Quantify Merlin AI ROI to justify customization effort: Bosch praised 'integrated AI concept' and 'potential for future enhancements' but flagged 'not all funct
deltas.subject: Quantify Merlin AI ROI to justify customization effort: Bosch praised 'integrated AI concept' and 'potential for future enhancements' but flagged 'not all functionalities meet expectations 100% out of the box; huge potential for customization' (22 May). If competitor offers better out-of-box fit, Bosch chooses lower implementation effort. Rank-3 move: quantify Merlin agentic AI savings (procurement headcount reduction, cycle-time compression, automation %) and unified S2P TCO advantage vs best-of-breed competitors; present ROI case in RFP Round 2 response to justify customization investment.
